desc:
Amazed at the clever concealment, you proceed into a small room hidden from view
by the tapestry. As you enter, a feeling of dread washes over you like a wave.
Perhaps some ancient magic has left guardians to watch over the area. The single
room differs from the tunnels in both height and width, being on the smaller
side of both aspects. Only a single torch bracket adorns the walls of the place.
The floor is hard dirt and the walls are the same cold stone.
name: A Secret Vault

desc:
Walking further along the dim passage, no signs of life are yet apparent. This
is not surprising as the folk that inhabited this land are long gone. The
Dwarves long ago shut the mighty gates of Moria in fear of the dark lord. And
the kingdom of Noldor Elves, in the city of Ost-in-Edhil, is nothing but ruin
now. Yet evidence of their existence is here in this tunnel today. Discarded
weapons and decaying leather lie scattered about the floor. Even a ragged
tapestry is still clinging to the hooks that hold it to the wall.
name: A Concealed Passage

desc:
Pushing past the small opening you discovered, you find yourself in a dark
tunnel that at first glance, appears to be empty. Although there is no obvious
sign of inhabitants at this time, torch brackets on the wall as well as a few
broken barrels indicate that this once was a place used by humanoids. The walls
are cold and grey, made of simple stone blocks erected to keep dirt from
collapsing and blocking the tunnel. You quickly surmise that this must have been
an underground refuge for the Noldor Elves and Moria Dwarves that shared the
dwelling above.
name: A Concealed Tunnel

desc:
The steps down from the kitchen are smooth and steep. At the bottom you find
yourself in a neat storage area, long since empty of goods. As the temperature
down here is much colder, caused by being surrounded on all sides by the cool
damp earth, you feel this area was used for cold storage. As evidence of this, a
wine barrel sits unused in the corner. Hard earth makes up the floor, while grey
blocks of stone form the walls of the cellar. The only exit appears to be back
up into the kitchen.
name: Cellar of the Hall

desc:
Cutting this room out of the hill around it must have been no easy chore. Though
not solid rock, the earth around here is hard packed, with trees and roots to
inhibit excavation. The task was done however, and a small kitchen was the
result of the work. Leading upwards to the outside, a small vent ascends from
the ceiling. The great brick ovens used so many years ago, are smashed into
barely recognisable rubble. A massive fire pit sunk into the middle of the floor
is the most notable attraction. Being at least a square yard in area, it must
have held a vast amount of wood.
name: The Kitchen

desc:
Moving further into the spacious meeting hall, you are not surprised to see it
in shambles. Where once a large table stood, probably holding up to fifty
guests, now only holds a pile of ashes and one of the table's wooden legs. A
couple of chairs are overturned on the far side of the room, gathering much dust
as the wood structure slowly decays around them. Off to the south is another
room, though not half as large as this one. A bit of ripped cloth on the wall
suggests there were a few tapestries covering the logs.
name: The Feasting Hall

desc:
Stepping inside the abandoned building, you wonder why it was built. Since this
place is located west of the Misty Mountains, and east of old Ost-in-Edhil, you
correctly surmise that this was a central meeting point for the Noldor Elves and
the Dwarves of Moria. When business had to be discussed, or trade to be
accomplished, it could have easily taken place here, as it would serve as
equidistant from the two remote places. Of course, if the meeting was of great
importance, it would undoubtedly have been held in either of the two more
principal locations.
name: A Large Foyer

desc:
A weather-beaten cave provides some form of shelter here. Dirt has been tracked
in and out of this cave, and wooden beams shore up its walls. The trail of dirt
lead outside seems to come the east and west. Although the cave seems naturally
formed, traces of craftsmanship can be seen about the edges.
name: Weatherworn Guard Cave

desc:
A great slab of rock juts out from the wall to the east, making passage to the
north almost impossible. Only a narrow gap between the west wall of the tunnel
and this protrusion can be seen. At the centre of this passage an ancient statue
stands proudly yet is quite worn. The form of the statue can just barely be
distinguished as that of an elf.
name: Stone Courtyard

desc:
A couple of rough, wooden practice targets have been set here with deep gashes
in them indicating heavy use. Straw lines the floor, tossed here and there
making for a soft footing. The granite walls of the cave have been skillfuly
carved and are smooth and perfectly vertical.
name: Training Room

desc:
A stone causeway perfectly level and smooth has been laid here. Dust and rubble
have been swept to the edges of the walkway. On the walls hang heavy iron
torches, flooding the area in a small circle of light. Here an intersections of
paths branch off leading east, north and south.
name: Ancient Stone Walkway

desc:
The path travels north and west, while to the south an adjoining area is lit
with a dull glow. Torches have been hung on the walls providing both light and
warmth to the passage, and casting flickering shadows upon the walls. Rocks and
rubble line the sides of the walkway.
name: Ancient Stone Walkway

desc:
Dimly lit by torches, a staircase can be seen here leading down. No railings are
attached to it, and it is made of solid rock. Masterfully they are carved yet
very old. They wind downward in a spiral, while to the north the tunnel
continues up a gentle slope. Thick dust lies here as if it is seldom disturbed.
name: Crumbling Stairs

desc:
The steps disappear above and below in a spiral. The granite from which they
have been cut, once coarse, are now worn smooth with the footsteps of those who
have come before. A black vein of obsidian runs along the passage, winding
around the staircase like a vine.
name: On an Unfinished Stair

desc:
The staircase winds its way up and down with little to be seen in either
direction due to the spiralling nature of the steps. Small stones mixed with
slivers of shale are strewn about the stair, making careful foot placement an
absolute must. The air is dry and musty, with particles of dust floating in the
stillness.
name: On an Unfinished Stair

desc:
Streaks of limestone slash their way horizontally across the cave walls, while a
blank greyness envelops the rest of the rock. The tunnel floor has been cut
quite smooth. Traces of decorative carvings are barely visible among the walls,
almost entirely faded with the passage of time.
name: Dilapitated Tunnel

desc:
Clearly fallen into disrepair, this tunnel leads off into the east and west.
Grey stone dominates this cavern, with hardly another colour to be seen; an
ocean of granite laps its way up the walls and ceiling. Small shelves of rock
seem to have been cut into the walls for some forgotten purpose.
name: Disused Tunnel

desc:
The walkway makes a sharp bend from south to west, visible through the light
cast by a single lit torch. On the eastern walls a great white rock, pale and
almost luminous, juts out from the wall like a sword. It seems as if chunks of
it are missing as if someone has taken a pick to it at some point.
name: Ancient Stone Walkway

desc:
The passage walls bear no mark of pick or chisel, yet are smooth and quite
polished. The tunnel here continues east and west, paved with wooden planks.
Torches light the hallway and the sound of their crackling echoes off the stone
walls. A light breeze can sometimes be detected coming from somewhere to the
east.
name: Ancient Stone Walkway

desc:
A wooden platform spans from the east to the south, offering footing over a
gaping chasm that opens down below. Here and there the floorboards are missing,
and the bridge-like structure creaks and groans as weight is placed upon it.
Nothing can be seen when peering down into the blackness of the chasm.
name: Bridge over a Pool of Darkness

desc:
The tunnel narrows here as it traverses north and south, leaving little room to
wriggle through. The air is hot and stuffy, drafting up from the depths of the
mountain itself from a crack that runs perpendicular across the tunnel. Some
mounds of rubble have been pushed aside to clear space through the tunnel.
name: Back Passage

desc:
A long, black fissure can be seen in the southwest corner of this cave leading,
to an unending void of darkness. Wooden planks are stacked in front of it
helping to bar anything from falling through. Torches help light the area
casting flickering shadows which dance mysteriously on the walls.
name: Ancient Stone Walkway

desc:
The stone walkway extends to the west and east but branching in other directions
as well. To the north and south, dark metal doors contrast with the rock wall.
The south opens up into a larger hall where some smoke can be seen flowing in.
Pelts of various animals line the walls.
name: Ancient Stone Walkway

desc:
The cold floor and walls of this prison cell are comprised of solid grey rock.
Desperate dig marks can be seen pocked across the walls from previous prisoners.
Some soiled straw covers the ground while many sets of iron chains and shackles
can be seen fastened to the walls.
name: Gloomy Prison Cell

desc:
A thick plume of smoke billows upwards from a rough campfire, exiting from a
small hole in the ceiling, which allows for some respite from the difficult to
breathe air. A few bunkbeds made of wooden stand around the edges of the room,
as if bolted to the walls. Heavy, warm bear hides have been strung up around the
dormitory.
name: Smoky Sleeping Quarters

desc:
A long wooden table with iron fittings on each corner stands in the centre of
the room. Chairs are gathered around it. A single lantern hangs above, casting
as much shadow as it does light. In the back corner, a roaring hearth spreads a
warm glow throughout the room as spits of meat roast over the open flame.
name: Mess Hall
